lines.gurmukhi: ਅਸਤਿ ਚਰਮ ਬਿਸਟਾ ਕੇ ਮੂੰਦੇ ਦੁਰਗੰਧ ਹੀ ਕੇ ਬੇਢੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: asat charam bisattaa ke moonde duragandh hee ke bedte |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You are nothing more than a bundle of bones, wrapped in skin, filled with manure; you give off such a rotten smell! ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਲਤ ਕਤ ਟੇਢੇ ਟੇਢੇ ਟੇਢੇ ॥
lines.translit: chalat kat ttedte ttedte ttedte |
lines.english: Why do you walk in that crooked, zig-zag way?

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨ ਜਪਹੁ ਕਵਨ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਭੂਲੇ ਤੁਮ ਤੇ ਕਾਲੁ ਨ ਦੂਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: raam na japahu kavan bhram bhoole tum te kaal na doore |
lines.english: You do not meditate on the Lord. What doubts have confused and deluded you? Death is not far away from you!

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਿ ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਰਾਖਹੁ ਰਹੈ ਅਵਸਥਾ ਪੂਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: anik jatan kar ihu tan raakhahu rahai avasathaa poore |2|
lines.english: Making all sorts of efforts, you manage to preserve this body, but it shall only survive until its time is up. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਨ ਕੀਆ ਕਛੂ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਕਿਆ ਕੋ ਕਰੈ ਪਰਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: aapan keea kachhoo na hovai kiaa ko karai paraanee |
lines.english: By one's own efforts, nothing is done. What can the mere mortal accomplish?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਭੇਟੈ ਏਕੋ ਨਾਮੁ ਬਖਾਨੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jaa tis bhaavai satigur bhettai eko naam bakhaanee |3|
lines.english: When it pleases the Lord, the mortal meets the True Guru, and chants the Name of the One Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਲੂਆ ਕੇ ਘਰੂਆ ਮਹਿ ਬਸਤੇ ਫੁਲਵਤ ਦੇਹ ਅਇਆਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: balooaa ke gharooaa meh basate fulavat deh aeaane |
lines.english: You live in a house of sand, but you still puff up your body - you ignorant fool!

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਜਿਹ ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਿਓ ਬੂਡੇ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਸਿਆਨੇ ॥੪॥੪॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer jih raam na chetio boodde bahut siaane |4|4|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, those who do not remember the Lord may be very clever, but they still drown. ||4||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਟੇਢੀ ਪਾਗ ਟੇਢੇ ਚਲੇ ਲਾਗੇ ਬੀਰੇ ਖਾਨ ॥
lines.translit: ttedtee paag ttedte chale laage beere khaan |
lines.english: Your turban is crooked, and you walk crooked; and now you have started chewing betel leaves.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਉ ਭਗਤਿ ਸਿਉ ਕਾਜੁ ਨ ਕਛੂਐ ਮੇਰੋ ਕਾਮੁ ਦੀਵਾਨ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhaau bhagat siau kaaj na kachhooai mero kaam deevaan |1|
lines.english: You have no use at all for loving devotional worship; you say you have business in court. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਬਿਸਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ ਅਭਿਮਾਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: raam bisaario hai abhimaan |
lines.english: In your egotistical pride, you have forgotten the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਨਿਕ ਕਾਮਨੀ ਮਹਾ ਸੁੰਦਰੀ ਪੇਖਿ ਪੇਖਿ ਸਚੁ ਮਾਨਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kanik kaamanee mahaa sundaree pekh pekh sach maan |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Gazing upon your gold, and your very beautiful wife, you believe that they are permanent. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਾਲਚ ਝੂਠ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਮਹਾ ਮਦ ਇਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਅਉਧ ਬਿਹਾਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: laalach jhootth bikaar mahaa mad ih bidh aaudh bihaan |
lines.english: You are engrossed in greed, falsehood, corruption and great arrogance. Your life is passing away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਅੰਤ ਕੀ ਬੇਰ ਆਇ ਲਾਗੋ ਕਾਲੁ ਨਿਦਾਨਿ ॥੨॥੫॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer ant kee ber aae laago kaal nidaan |2|5|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, at the very last moment, death will come and seize you, you fool! ||2||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਤਨਕੁ ਖਟੀਆ ਗਠੀਆ ਮਟੀਆ ਸੰਗਿ ਨ ਕਛੁ ਲੈ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: eitanak khatteea gattheea matteea sang na kachh lai jaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: With so much wealth and cash and buried treasure, still, he cannot take anything with him. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਰਿ ਦਿਨ ਅਪਨੀ ਨਉਬਤਿ ਚਲੇ ਬਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: chaar din apanee naubat chale bajaae |
lines.english: The mortal beats the drum for a few days, and then he must depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਹਰੀ ਬੈਠੀ ਮਿਹਰੀ ਰੋਵੈ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਲਉ ਸੰਗਿ ਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: diharee baitthee miharee rovai duaarai lau sang maae |
lines.english: Sitting on the threshhold, his wife weeps and wails; his mother accompanies him to the outer gate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਹਟ ਲਗਿ ਸਭੁ ਲੋਗੁ ਕੁਟੰਬੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਹੰਸੁ ਇਕੇਲਾ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: marahatt lag sabh log kuttanb mil hans ikelaa jaae |1|
lines.english: All the people and relatives together go to the crematorium, but the swan-soul must go home all alone. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੈ ਸੁਤ ਵੈ ਬਿਤ ਵੈ ਪੁਰ ਪਾਟਨ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਨ ਦੇਖੈ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: vai sut vai bit vai pur paattan bahur na dekhai aae |
lines.english: Those children, that wealth, that city and town - he shall not come to see them again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤੁ ਕਬੀਰੁ ਰਾਮੁ ਕੀ ਨ ਸਿਮਰਹੁ ਜਨਮੁ ਅਕਾਰਥੁ ਜਾਇ ॥੨॥੬॥
lines.translit: kehat kabeer raam kee na simarahu janam akaarath jaae |2|6|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, why do you not meditate on the Lord? Your life is uselessly slipping away! ||2||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਕੇਦਾਰਾ ਬਾਣੀ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ਜੀਉ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: raag kedaaraa baanee ravidaas jeeo kee |
lines.english: Raag Kaydaaraa, The Word Of Ravi Daas Jee:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਟੁ ਕਰਮ ਕੁਲ ਸੰਜੁਗਤੁ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: khatt karam kul sanjugat hai har bhagat hiradai naeh |
lines.english: One who performs the six religious rituals and comes from a good family, but who does not have devotion to the Lord in his heart,

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਨਾਰਬਿੰਦ ਨ ਕਥਾ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੁਪਚ ਤੁਲਿ ਸਮਾਨਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: charanaarabind na kathaa bhaavai supach tul samaan |1|
lines.english: one who does not appreciate talk of the Lord's Lotus Feet, is just like an outcaste, a pariah. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਸੁ ਜਾਤਿ ਤੇ ਕਿਹ ਪਦਹਿ ਅਮਰਿਓ ਰਾਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਬਿਸੇਖ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kis jaat te kih padeh amario raam bhagat bisekh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: From such a low social status, what a high status he obtained! Devotional worship to the Lord is sublime! ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੇ ਨ ਬਾਲਮੀਕਹਿ ਦੇਖ ॥
lines.translit: kaahe na baalameekeh dekh |
lines.english: Why do you not look at Baalmeek?

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੇ ਚਿਤ ਚੇਤਿ ਚੇਤ ਅਚੇਤ ॥
lines.translit: re chit chet chet achet |
lines.english: Be conscious, be conscious, be conscious, O my unconscious mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਗੁ ਬਪੁਰਾ ਕਿਆ ਸਰਾਹੈ ਤੀਨਿ ਲੋਕ ਪ੍ਰਵੇਸ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: log bapuraa kiaa saraahai teen lok praves |2|
lines.english: See how the poor people praise him! His praise extends throughout the three worlds. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਆਨ ਸਤ੍ਰੁ ਅਜਾਤੁ ਸਭ ਤੇ ਕ੍ਰਿਸ੍ਨ ਲਾਵੈ ਹੇਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: suaan satru ajaat sabh te krisan laavai het |
lines.english: The killer of dogs, the lowest of all, was lovingly embraced by Krishna.

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸੇ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਨਿਸਤਰੇ ਤੂ ਕਿਉ ਨ ਤਰਹਿ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ॥੩॥੧॥
lines.translit: aise duramat nisatare too kiau na tareh ravidaas |3|1|
lines.english: Even such evil-minded beings were emancipated. Why should you not also be saved, O Ravi Daas? ||3||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜਾਮਲੁ ਪਿੰਗੁਲਾ ਲੁਭਤੁ ਕੁੰਚਰੁ ਗਏ ਹਰਿ ਕੈ ਪਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: ajaamal pingulaa lubhat kunchar ge har kai paas |
lines.english: Ajaamal, Pingulaa, Lodhia and the elephant went to the Lord.
